description_long: This is a vintage-style electric fan with a sturdy, round metal base and a cylindrical motor housing at its center. The fan features four wide, dark-colored blades encased in a circular wire guard with a geometric, open-frame design. The rear of the motor housing displays a vented cover, likely for cooling, and a small metallic reservoir or oil cup is attached to the side, suggesting an older, possibly oil-lubricated mechanism. The fan's body and base are predominantly black with metallic accents, giving it a robust and industrial appearance. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal, with visible screws and joints reinforcing its durability. No visible control panel or power cord is present in the images, and the fan is designed to sit securely on flat surfaces.
description: A vintage black metal electric fan with four wide blades, encased in a circular wire guard and mounted on a sturdy round base. The design is industrial and robust, with metallic accents and a small side reservoir.
description_view_0: Front view showing four wide blades and the circular wire guard, with the base and central hub clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical motor housing, the small metallic reservoir, and the depth of the blade guard.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the vented cover of the motor housing and the back of the blade guard.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the motor housing and the wire guard's profile, with the base and support structure visible.
attribute_blade_guard_design: The blade guard is an open-frame circular wire structure with radial and circumferential supports, made of metal and designed to prevent accidental contact with the blades while allowing airflow.
